pregame_reference.det_market_ml_range: approximately -115 to -135 before tip across books shown

live_halftime_context.live_spread_market: ORL wins by over 4.5 at 49%, No at 51%

investments.selection: No - Orlando wins by over 4.5 points

investment_grade.market_capture_grade: B+ on DET price, C on hedge timing

actual_vs_model_delta.spread_delta: Model DET by 4.7 vs actual ORL by 8; directional miss of 12.7 points
actual_vs_model_delta.primary_miss_reason: Detroit side was mispriced by model because Cade turnover volatility and Orlando starter efficiency/rebounding dominance overwhelmed the projected Detroit shot-quality edge.

Cade Cunningham.note: Primary failure node; usage stayed elite but 9 turnovers created live-ball and possession drag.

Ausar Thompson.note: Strong defensive/event impact and rim efficiency; not enough to offset Cade turnover drag.

Jalen Duren.note: Foul pressure capped minutes and limited closing stability.

Paolo Banchero.note: Primary all-around engine; created FT pressure and rebound stability.

Wendell Carter Jr..note: Biggest model miss in rebound/possession structure; ORL interior board advantage validated.

Desmond Bane.note: Shotmaking spike sustained from halftime; key reason ORL margin cleared 4.5.

Jalen Suggs.note: Volatile but contributed pressure and scoring.

Franz Wagner.note: Best plus-minus starter; stabilized ORL units.

det_starting_lineup.note: Starting group actually won its minutes, but non-starter and late-game volatility plus Cade turnovers broke the full-game side.

orl_starting_lineup.note: ORL starting lineup was a high-efficiency, high-rebound-possession engine.

hit_miss_reason.det_ml_loss: The pregame DET ML edge failed because the model over-weighted Detroit recent form and shot-quality profile while under-weighting Cade Cunningham turnover volatility, Duren foul/minute risk, and Orlando's starter rebound/3PT ceiling.
hit_miss_reason.no_orl_4_5_loss: The live hedge failed because Orlando's halftime edge was more structural than temporary: Bane's shooting remained impactful, Paolo/WCJ controlled possessions, and Orlando won the glass by 6 with 14 offensive rebounds.
hit_miss_reason.total_read: Pregame total projection was accurate directionally. Model mean 216.1 vs actual 218. The total was not the failure point; side/margin was.

model_patch_recommendations.description: Downgrade side/ML confidence when a high-usage primary creator shows elevated turnover risk against strong point-of-attack pressure.
model_patch_recommendations.trigger: Primary ballhandler projected usage high and opponent has multiple pressure defenders; in-game threshold 4+ TO by halftime or pregame volatility flag.

model_patch_recommendations.description: Downgrade team side when center rebound/rim-protection edge depends on a player with foul-risk matchup against a high-FT pressure frontcourt.
model_patch_recommendations.trigger: Center projected 30+ minutes but matchup opponent draws fouls through PF/C actions.

model_patch_recommendations.description: Upgrade Orlando side/team total/rebound markets when Banchero-Bane-WCJ-Franz-Suggs unit projects stable minutes and Jonathan Isaac absence does not reduce core starter minutes.
model_patch_recommendations.trigger: ORL starters confirmed with WCJ 34+ minute path and Bane/Paolo 36+ minute path.

model_patch_recommendations.description: Do not hedge against a favorite/leader solely on spread inflation if the halftime lead is supported by rebounding, FT pressure, and sustained lineup efficiency rather than pure shooting noise.
model_patch_recommendations.trigger: Halftime leader has positive rebound margin, OREB advantage, and opponent primary creator has 5+ turnovers.

final_verdict.summary: DET@ORL Game 3 was a side-model miss, not a total-model miss. Detroit captured a good market price pregame but the model failed to properly penalize Cade turnover volatility, Duren foul risk, and Orlando's starter-group rebounding/shotmaking ceiling. Orlando's 113-105 win validated starter-unit dominance, WCJ possession control, Paolo creation, and Bane shooting volatility.
final_verdict.backtest_value: High. This game should train the model to avoid overconfident ML exposure when primary ballhandler turnover risk and opponent starter-unit possession dominance are active.
